plot_beats.beat_text: [mode:teacher vessel:the mechanic who co-regulates the pilot / wound] the pilot forces the sync and burns out — apparatus: doctrine delivered as care — the mechanic reads the pilot's body, names the cost. Teacher/musician is NEVER named; the vessel carries the mode diegetically.
Wide establishing shot of the rooftop overlooking the city. Dawn light, stillness before the day.
Kai appears in silhouette against the rooftop overlooking the city. Their posture tells a story before any word is spoken.
Close on Kai's hands — fidgeting, gripping, or still. The body knows what the mind hasn't admitted.
Kai walks through the familiar space. Everything looks the same. Something has shifted.
A small detail catches Kai's eye — a crack in the wall, a wilting plant, a clock stopped at the wrong time.
Sora enters the frame without warning. Kai stiffens.
The first exchange. Words say one thing. Eyes say another. Neither acknowledges the gap.
Kai alone again. They exhale. The room holds the echo.
A memory surfaces — not as flashback but as body sensation. Kai's shoulders rise.
Night falls over the rooftop overlooking the city. Kai at the window. The reflection stares back.
SILENCE: The world breathes. No dialogue. No action. Just the space between heartbeats.
Kai makes a small choice — turns left instead of right, picks up a forgotten object, opens a closed door.

[mode:teacher vessel:the mechanic who co-regulates the pilot / turn] the mechanic: it knew before you did; settle, don't override — apparatus: doctrine delivered as care — the mechanic reads the pilot's body, names the cost. Teacher/musician is NEVER named; the vessel carries the mode diegetically.
Kai faces Sora across a charged space. Neither moves first.
Close on Kai's face as realization hits — the pattern they've been blind to.
Kai tries the old response. It doesn't work anymore. The body rejects the habit.
A confrontation builds. Sora pushes. Kai almost breaks — then catches themselves.
Kai alone, processing. The walls feel closer. Or maybe they always were this close.
An unexpected kindness from a stranger. Kai doesn't know how to receive it.
Kai speaks truth for the first time — halting, imperfect, real.
The cost of the truth lands. Silence follows. Neither person looks away.
Kai walks a new path. Each step deliberate. The ground feels different.
SILENCE: A breath held and released. The world continues. Something has loosened.
Sora shows a crack in their armor. For one panel, the shadow looks human.
Kai returns to a familiar place but sees it differently. The change is internal.

[mode:teacher vessel:the mechanic who co-regulates the pilot / renewal] pilot and machine move from a settled body — apparatus: doctrine delivered as care — the mechanic reads the pilot's body, names the cost. Teacher/musician is NEVER named; the vessel carries the mode diegetically.
Kai and Sora face each other for the final time. This isn't about winning.
Everything Kai has learned crystallizes in one moment. The body moves before the mind.
Kai chooses the harder path — not the dramatic one, but the honest one.
Sora falters. Not defeated — seen. The shadow recognized is no longer a shadow.
The world holds still. What needed to happen has happened. What needed to break has broken.
SILENCE: The deepest pause. Three panels of nothing but breath and light.
Kai stands in the aftermath. Changed not by victory but by surrender.
A hand extended — to Sora, to self, to the reader. Integration, not triumph.
Kai speaks the last words of the arc. Quiet. Unforced. True.
the rooftop overlooking the city at peace. The light has changed. Dawn or dusk — it doesn't matter. What matters is the stillness.
Final panel: Kai's eyes. Not resolved. Not healed. Awake.
SILENCE: The story ends where the reader begins. No summary. No lesson. Just the echo.

mode_vessel.vessel: the mechanic who co-regulates the pilot
mode_vessel.vessel_desc: doctrine delivered as care — the mechanic reads the pilot's body, names the cost

beats.wound: the pilot forces the sync and burns out
beats.turn: the mechanic: it knew before you did; settle, don't override
beats.renewal: pilot and machine move from a settled body
